The marketplace of jihad was firmly established under Banu Umayyah; they had no occupation but that. The word of Islam had risen across the east and west of the earth, its land and its seas. They subdued kufr and its people, and the hearts of the mushrikin were filled with fear of the Muslims. Wherever the Muslims turned, they seized it. Within their armies and expeditions were the righteous, the awliya, and the scholars from among the senior Tabi'un; in every army there was a great contingent through whom Allah granted victory to His religion. 
(Ibn Kathir, al-Bidaya wa al-Nihaya, vol. 9)

Ibn Khaldun said:

Banu Abd Manaf among Quraysh possessed a share of numbers and honour that none of the other clans of Quraysh could rival. Their two branches, Banu Umayyah and Banu Hashim, were both a single group tracing back to Abd Manaf and affiliating themselves with him. Quraysh recognised this and sought leadership from them; however, Banu Umayyah were greater in number than Banu Hashim and had more men, and strength lies in numbers. As the poet said: Indeed, strength belongs to the more numerous. (Ibn Khaldun, Tarikh).

And that's the beginningKhomeini's tomb also started small until it turned into one of the most lavish shrines and mausoleums ever erected in the name of Islam. The shrine for Nasrallat and Hashem Safeeh al-Din is supposed to look like this. Images of the plans show an elaborate plaza with a central dome where he will be interred. The site will be festooned with Catholic-esque saint images of him, literally opposing the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah, peace be updated jnhim, and his household, who commanded us to level graves and keep them as plain as possible. It is expected to be built on the road leading to Beirut International Airport, in the Bourj el-Barajneh neighbourhood of Beirut.

#iran #hezbollah #hizbullah #hizbushshaytan

This is the shrine (!) the Khomeinist Rafidi Shia have quickly set up for Ali Larijani, i.e. it is still in progress (if you want to see how their idolatrous shrines look in the final stage, just search for Khomeinis shrine and mausoleum, which resembles more a Persian Sassanian palace than a modest, simple Muslim grave). 

The Shariah violations are so many that even trying to keep this short still turns into an essay:

1Raised, built-up grave  clear violation of the Sunnah (graves must be simple and only slightly raised)  like Hindu samadhi structures / Christian tomb monuments (He forbade plastering graves and building over them  Sahih Muslim)

2Shrine-style enclosure  turns a grave into a place of veneration, not burial  like Hindu shrine spaces / church-style memorial areas (Do not take graves as places of worship  Sahih Muslim)

3Huge portrait above the grave  images tied to veneration are forbidden  like Christian icon worship / saint imagery (Umm Salamah reported churches in Abyssinia with images over graves; the Prophet, peace be upon him, said: Those are the WORST of creation  Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim)

4Flowers placed as offerings  ritualised, not from the Sunnah  like Hindu offerings at shrines / graveside rituals in churches (no established Sunnah practice of ritual offerings at graves; the Prophet, peace be upon him, forbade introducing practices into the religion  Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim)

5The whole setup is theatrical and attention-seeking  opposite of Islamic humility in death  like Hindu devotional sites / Christian saint shrines (Do not exaggerate in praising me  Sahih al-Bukhari)

6Encourages attachment to the dead instead of Allah  exactly the path the Prophet, peace be upon him, warned leads to shirk  identical to Hindu guru veneration / Christian saint intercession culture (O Allah, do not make my grave an idol that is worshipped  Musnad Ahmad)

7Grave turned into a focal point  not remembrance, but glorification  same model as shrine-based religions (Do not sit on graves nor pray towards them  Sahih Muslim)

Bottom line:

This is not a Muslim grave. It is a shrine culture resembling polytheistic traditions rather than what Islam taught, and it was none but the Ahl al-Bayt, peace be upon them, from whom it is authentically narrated that all graves must be levelled and kept simple, like those in al-Baqi, which were not destroyed as grave-worshippers claim, but restored to their original humble form.

"LIBERAL DEMOCRAT COUNCILLOR JAILED FOR SEXUALLY ASSAULTING CHILD

A councillor from Gloucestershire has been jailed after being found guilty of sexually assaulting a child.

Paul Ockelton, from Innsworth, near Gloucester, denied sexually assaulting a girl under the age of 13 but was found guilty following a four-day trial in March.

The former Liberal Democrat member who represented Innsworth on Tewkesbury Borough Council, was jailed for 18 months.

He was also placed on the sex offenders register for 10 years and made the subject of a sexual harm prevention order.

Ockelton carried out his assault in November 2021.

He sat his victim on his lap and asked her "do you like this?" while he carried out the assault, the court was told.

Ockelton denied he had touched her inappropriately, claiming he had been tickling her tummy and brushing her hair, but a jury rejected his defence.

The victims mum told the court: I am saddened that my daughter's wellbeing and confidence has been so badly shattered by his actions.

No-one has the right to touch my daughter and it is something that will affect her for the rest of her life. She is the most amazing and beautiful little girl.

Det Sgt Grace Craddock, of Gloucestershire Police, praised the girls bravery.

The child victim in this case has been unbelievably brave by letting her parents know what Ockelton had done to her, she said.

The strength shown by her family throughout this difficult process too has been incredible, and I commend them for working with us to ensure Ockelton has been convicted and now sentenced for his offending.

Tewkesbury Borough Councils Liberal Democrat leader Richard Stanley said it was a shocking and disturbing case and the authoritys thoughts are with the victim.

To be clear, Mr Ockelton withheld the fact that there were ongoing proceedings during his application process to stand as a councillor,"

"There are men whose voices only rise when the winds of public opinion begin to blow, and there are men whose words precede the storm itself. Men who speak when it is difficult, when it is unpopular, when it carries a price. From the signs of a true Alim is not that he reacts to events, but that he forewarns the Ummah before the events even unfold. You can mention a calamity, a trial, a tribulation that has struck this Ummah over the past four decades  except that a record exists, a reminder preserved, where Shaykh Ahmad had already spoken, already warned, already clarified. This is not coincidence by the way this is Basirh, insight granted by Allh to those who cling firmly to revelation.

Allh says:

         
O you who believe, if you fear Allh, He will grant you a criterion. (Surah al-Anfl 8:29)

This furqn is not merely knowledge it is the ability to distinguish truth from falsehood when the masses are confused, when emotions are high, when deception is widespread. And this is what we witnessed. 

When the issue of Falastn suddenly became a global trend, when voices rose and slogans filled the air, Shaykh Ahmad did not need to scramble to speak because he had already addressed it years prior, almost a decade earlier, clarifying its reality, its roots, and the correct Islamic stance upon it. While others rode the wave, he had already charted the course.

The Messenger of Allh  said:

             
Indeed, Allh does not take away knowledge by snatching it from the people, but He takes away knowledge by taking the scholars. 

So look carefully, when a scholar speaks consistently, courageously, and with foresight, this is a nimah that must be recognized before it is lost.

When people rushed toward the ballot boxes, placing hope in systems not built upon the Shariah, he had already spoken  not once, but repeatedly  exposing the foundational deviation in such systems, warning against the dilution of Tawhid and the subtle normalization of shirk in governance. While others justified, softened, or remained silent, he stood firm upon principle.

And when the tides of confusion intensified  in the recent affairs involving the Rfidah and the situation tied to Iran  again, this was not something new to him. Long before many understood in the West, he had already exposed their history of oppression, their crimes in Shm, their treachery against Ahl al-Sunnah. He spoke when it was not trending, when it did not bring followers, when it did not align with popular narratives.

When the blood of the Muslims in Ghouta was spilled, when oppression crushed the weak, his voice was present. When the tyranny of the Taghut of Egypt, Sisi, unfolded, he did not veil the truth. 

This is the reality of the scholar who fears Allh not the reactions of people, nor the consequences of speaking truth.

And here lies the difference between those who inherit the legacy of the Prophets, and those who bend under pressure, who remain silent to preserve status, or who speak only when it is safe. The Murjiah and their ilk wish that their scholars possessed even a fraction of this courage but truth is not granted to every tongue, nor is firmness gifted to every heart."

"A questioner asks: What is the ruling on offering congratulations and gifts on the occasion of Valentines Day, which falls in mid-February each year?

All praise is due to Allah, and peace and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah and upon his family and companions. As for what follows:

This holiday is a festival of the kuffar and Mushrikeen. Even if it is called Valentines Day, it is a festival claimed by the Greeks, an ancient festival for a deity they worshipped besides Allah. 

Whether this holiday was for a deity they worshipped or merely for what they call and claim to be love, then congratulating others on it, celebrating this day, and honoring it is forbidden and not permissible.

We, the people of Islam, have only two Eids: Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha. It is not permissible to congratulate others for any Eid besides these two. So congratulating others on this day and honoring it is not allowed; it is forbidden. You are not permitted to congratulate anyone on it, nor are you permitted to accept congratulations from anyone on this day. Rather, it is obligatory upon you and every Muslim to reject anyone who congratulates you on this day and to clarify to people that congratulating others on this day is forbidden and not permissible, and that it is a form of imitating the Kuffar and Mushrikeen in their false festivals, which are based on falsehood, disbelief, immorality, and wickedness.

How can they claim it is a day of love and magnify love while they are among the most hostile people toward Islam and Muslims, waging war against the religion of Allah and against the people of Islam? A Muslim should not be deceived by their labels or slogans. 

These Kuffar and wicked people are among the furthest from what is called love. They are the ones killing us, killing our brothers in Syria, Iraq, and everywhere, yet they claim this day is Valentines Day.

Therefore, it is not permissible to congratulate others on this day, nor is it permissible for a Muslim to honor it, give gifts on it, or accept gifts on it. It is a forbidden day; honoring it, celebrating it, and offering congratulations for such false festivals is forbidden.

We have only two Eids: Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha, and these are the ones in which it is permissible to rejoice.

Likewise, commercial shops that sell gifts such as flowers, teddy bears, and similar items on this day, some people even change their pages or displays to red or use symbols associated with celebrating this day, selling flowers or anything that assists in honoring this day is not permissible. It is forbidden and is considered helping in falsehood. 

Allah says: Cooperate in righteousness and piety, and do not cooperate in sin and transgression.

Whoever sells such flowers with the intention of honoring that day, his sale is forbidden, and the money he earns from it is unlawful. Every body nourished by unlawful earnings, the Fire is more deserving of it. So it is not permissible to take such money, nor to sell flowers, change displays, alter slogans, or use symbols such as roses or the color red for this day, for these are forbidden and reprehensible matters.

It is obligatory upon a Muslim to honor the symbols of Allah, to honor the religion of Allah, to show disavowal of these Kuffar and wicked people, and to distinguish himself from them in their festivals and their false, misguided beliefs. For resemblance in outward matters leads to resemblance in inward matters.

We ask Allah for well-being and safety. 

Shaykh Khalid al Fulaij (may Allah free him)"

"The most exalted day of the year for du has dawned  the blessed Day of Arafah.
Prepare your heart and your du from now, for this is a day when du are answered, hopes are fulfilled, sins are forgiven, faults are pardoned, and souls are freed from the Fire.

Approach this day with profound humility. Renounce reliance on your own might, and acknowledge that all strength and power belong solely to Allah. Embrace the truth of human frailty and surrender entirely to Him.

Let your supplications flow abundantly and unceasingly. Immerse your heart and tongue in dhikr, du, and heartfelt recitation of the Quran. It is reported that among the pious predecessors, some would spend the Day of Arafah seated in deep remembrance and prayer, yearning for the mercy of their Lord.

Make your du lengthy and persistent. Return again and again with your most intimate personal requests  and do not forget the oppressed and the afflicted among this Ummah.

Know with Yaqeen that when Allah grants, His generosity surpasses all imagination. The arrows of du on the Day of Arafah  by Allahs infinite permission  never miss their mark. 

Whoever remains steadfast in du shall be granted enduring joy and fiyah. 

The great scholar Ibn Abd al-Barr said:

The supplication on the Day of Arafah is better than on any other day and the du of Arafah is, in most cases, accepted.

And inLatif al-Marif, Ibn Rajab relates that one of the righteous said:

By Allah, I have never made a du on the Day of Arafah, and a year passed without seeing it answered  as clearly as the break of dawn.

The Day of Arafah is a majestic and awe-inspiring day  a day when the signs of Allahs mercy are clearly manifest.
It presents a sacred opportunity to renew ones faith, realign ones path, and make a sincere, heartfelt resolution to repent and submit wholly to Allah  not only for the coming year, but for a lifetime.

As virtuous as Laylat al-Qadr is, we do not know which night among the last ten it truly is.But we do know the Day of Arafah  the 9th of Dhul-Hijjah  and this year, it falls onThursday, tomorrow.

On Laylat al-Qadr, Jibrl and the angels descend, and that is a tremendous virtue.But on the Day of Arafah,Allah descendsin a manner befitting His Majesty.

It is a day when the signs ofTawdare renewed and exalted, and when pure servitude to Allah alone  without any partner  is fully realized.

That is why it is mentioned that among the recommended supplications for the Day of Arafah is a du ofTawd:

L ilha illa Allh, wadahu l sharka lah, lahu al-mulku wa lahu al-amd, wa huwa al kulli shayin qadr.There is no god but Allah, alone without partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He has power over all things.

One must be vigilant not to fall short on this sacred day  for it is a moment that cannot be reclaimed, an opportunity that may never come again.

Dont forget to fast on this blessed day (if you are not performing Hajj), for it expiates the sins of the previous year and the year to come.

Ya Allah, let not the Day of Arafah pass except that You have lifted from us every burden, eased our affairs, and answered our du.

Ya Allah, Most Compassionate, swiftly grant freedom to the imprisoned Muslims wherever they may be, and free our sisters enduring hardship and suffering in the camps.
Ya Allah, Protector of the helpless, shield our children, our brothers, and our sisters in Gaza and elsewhere from harm and despair.
Ya Allah, bring down theawghtwho spread corruption and injustice far and wide  those who conspired with the disbelievers against the believers in Gaza and in Baghuz.
Ya Allah, grant the righteous and sincereMuwaidna clear, resounding victory.
Ya Allah, Most Merciful, envelop us all in Your boundless pardon, infinite gentleness, and overwhelming mercy.

Ahmad Musa Jibril
Dhul-Hijjah 8, 1446"

"The war on Gaza has not ceased  it has merely morphed into grimmer, more insidious forms.

The bombs still rain from the sky. The children still perish. The siege tightens with each passing day.

And now, atop the shattered remnants of homes and lives, the assault has transformed into a war waged through starvation, through silence, through a deliberate and merciless neglect.

This is a war waged not only with missiles, but with malnutrition. With blocked aid. With the deafening quiet of a world that chooses to look away.As Gaza bleeds and crumbles beneath the weight of destruction, the world reclines on cushions of indifference.

Today, Gaza suffocates  in silence, in isolation.And yet it remains a gaping wound upon the conscience of this Ummah  festering with every moment of neglect and apathy.

"Whosoever honours the Symbols of Allah, then it is truly from the piety of the heart.  22:32

The first ten days of Dhul-Hijjah are among the most sacred moments in the Islamic calendar, days that Allah Himself has exalted. So honored are they that He swore by them in His Book, and Allah does not swear except by what is magnificent, noble, and elevated in rank.

In reverence to our Lord, we too honor these days as an expression of our veneration for Him.

These days are so weighty in virtue that time spent in worshipreciting the Quran, engaging in dhikr, and performing acts of devotionis more rewarding than Jihad in the path of Allah during any other time of the year, including Ramadan. The only exception is the one who goes forth in the path of Allah with his life and wealth, and returns with neither.

Simply recognizing the sanctity of these ten blessed days and being mindful of their immense virtue is itself a source of rewardso how tremendous must the reward be for those who engage in righteous deeds during them!

Whatever good deeds you door continue to doduring these ten days, add the intention of doing them out of reverence for what Allah has honored. The intention to honor Allahs sacred symbols increases the reward of your actions.

If youve missed the earlier days, dont lose heart, there is still time, and today is certainly not a day to let slip by.

Today is a day crowned with a triple honora day unlike any other:
It is part of the best days of the year: the first ten days of Dhul-Hijjah,
It falls on the best day of the week: Friday,
And within it lies the precious Hour of Response (Saat al-Ijabah), a time in which dus are answered.

These ten days are unlike any otherThey are days in which hearts are healed, dus are answered, and the gates of heaven are flung wide open. So pour out your soul in supplication, and anchor your heart to the door of the One who says, Be!and it is.

Seek out the sacred moments in which dus are most likely to be accepted.Within these days, a sincere call upon your LordY Rabbcould transform your life in ways you never imagined.

To honor and recognize the value of Allahs sacred seasons and symbols is a sign of a heart alive with taqw and mn. 

If you wish to measure the state of taqw in your heart, look to how much you honor what Allah has honored, by respecting their sanctity, filling them with righteous deeds, and avoiding sins.

Whoever misses these days has missed a great treasure and a tremendous reward.
Dont let these days slip awaythey are rare opportunities, windows of divine mercy that may never return.

Ahmad Musa Jibril 
Dhu al-Hijjah 3, 1446"

"POLICE SERGEANT AVOIDS JAIL AFTER MAKING INDECENT IMAGES OF KIDS.  

A Northamptonshire Police sergeant has received a 12month suspended prison sentence after admitting to making 80 indecent images of children, including material involving girls as young as four.

Ross Coleman, 42, was sentenced at Leicester Crown Court after pleading guilty to three offences of making indecent images between October 2016 and October 2018. Judge Rhona Campbell told him he had provided a market for images of the sexual abuse of children, stressing that such crimes directly contribute to the exploitation of vulnerable victims.

Police executed a warrant at Colemans former family home in Higham Ferrers. Officers seized several items, including his personal mobile phone, which contained thumbnail images linked to videos he had streamed to his Smart TV.

Over a twoyear period, Coleman downloaded 80 files depicting child sexual abuse. Fortyfive were classified as category A, the most serious level. A further 20 were category B and 15 category C. Investigators also found more than 100 additional files with similar names that had been accessed during the same timeframe.

Coleman was sentenced to 12 months imprisonment, suspended for 18 months. He must complete 120 hours of unpaid work and 35 days of rehabilitation activity. He is also subject to a Sexual Harm Prevention Order restricting his access to the internet.

Judge Campbell emphasised the realworld harm caused by such offending, telling the court that every image represented a child who had been abused and would carry the impact for life.

Coleman resigned from Northamptonshire Police in February. Following the conclusion of the criminal case, he will now face a gross misconduct hearing.

Detective Constable Jason Cullum, who led the investigation, said the outcome should serve as a warning to anyone who believes they can view indecent images without consequence. He noted that offenders risk losing their families, homes and employment, as Coleman did."

"When ilm is divorced from real struggle, and ibdah is severed from responsibility, devotion becomes performance and piety turns hollow. History shows that when knowledge is reduced to commentary, and worship is reduced to spectacle, the Ummah weakens, no matter how long the prayers or how loud the slogans.
Throughout Islamic history, the greatest scholars never flattered excess, theatrics, or escapism cloaked in religiosity. They exposed it, especially when it distracted the Ummah from its frontiers and obligations.

The Amr al-Muminn in adth, the scholar who encompassed every virtue except the companionship of the Prophet , Abdullh ibn alMubrak (r.a.) was once informed of certain ascetics who had taken up residence in Baghdad, becoming known for excess in worship and outward displays of devotion. He condemned them, as narrated by Jubaysh ibn Ab alWard, saying:

Oh you ascetics! Those who have donned wool and sacrificed animals merely to be counted among the worshippers.
Hold firmly to the frontiers and worship therein.
Baghdad is not the place for ascetics.
Baghdad is a resting-place for kings,
and a gathering-place and resort for hunters. (Trikh Baghdd) 

This was not a dismissal of worship, but a denunciation of misplaced devotion. Not a rebuke of asceticism, but of performative piety divorced from duty. Not an attack on spirituality, but on withdrawal while the Ummah bleeds.

Ibn al-Mubrak (r.a.) understood a truth many prefer to forget: worship that ignores obligation is not elevation, it is evasion. When oppression rages, frontiers collapse, and the Ummah is violated, retreating into comfortable piety is not neutrality, it is abandonment. True zuhd is not found in comfort, applause, or city circles. It is forged where responsibility is heaviest; where risk is real; where faith demands sacrifice, not spectatorship. It is worship that carries weight, consequence, and cost.

What would the great Abdullh ibn al-Mubrak (r.a.) say about us today?

He would not be impressed by packed Masjid that fall silent on injustice. He would not be moved by tears shed in du while hands remain idle from action. He would not praise those who debate theology endlessly yet recoil from responsibility. He would say that we have perfected the rituals and abandoned the burden. That we have learned to speak Islam fluently while fearing it's implications."
